mission: Convert production multimodal research, current Chinese/open-weight model pressure, and governed self-improving agent architecture feedback into benchmark variables for the Codex/flywheel local-model engine.

source_policy.scope: Seed corpus from verified public pages plus user-provided public feedback text.
source_policy.note: The Chinese-source lane is not exhaustive; it captures the first source set needed for benchmark scaffolding.

sources.title: LTX multimodal video generation model
observed_claims: LTX presents the model as production-grade and deployable via API, on-prem, or open weights.
LTX-2.3 is described as a 22B asymmetric dual-stream diffusion transformer with 14B video and 5B audio streams.
The page emphasizes open weights, local desktop execution, API integration, trainer tooling, predictable workflow control, and production use.
benchmark_implications: Add production multimodal workflow variables beyond text-only agent scoring.
Measure modality synchronization, workflow integration, local/on-prem deployability, and trainer or adapter paths.
Track license/deployment boundaries and cost-quality-latency tradeoffs.

sources.title: Lightricks/LTX-2.3 model card
observed_claims: The card describes LTX-2.3 as a diffusion-based audio-video foundation model with open weights and practical local execution focus.
The model card lists full, distilled, LoRA, and upscaler checkpoints.
The limitations section says the model is not intended for factual information and may miss prompts, amplify bias, or generate inappropriate content.
benchmark_implications: Separate factual-reasoning models from generative-world or media models.
Score model-card limitation labeling as part of deployment readiness.
Track checkpoint family, adapter path, upscaler path, and local runtime constraints.

sources.title: JUST-DUB-IT: Video Dubbing via Joint Audio-Visual Diffusion
sources.publisher: Lightricks / Tel Aviv University project page
observed_claims: The project frames a single joint audio-visual model as sufficient for video dubbing.
The method uses synthetic multilingual paired data, counterfactual inpainting, latent-aware masking, and in-context LoRA training.
Reported evaluation focuses on speaker identity, lip synchronization, naturalness, and robustness to complex motion.
benchmark_implications: Measure cross-modal synchronization and identity/style preservation as first-class metrics.
Score synthetic-data and counterfactual-generation pipelines by provenance and reproducibility.
Add adapter-training workflow evidence to model-improvement loops.

sources.title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B model card
observed_claims: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is an open-weight multimodal model with 35B total and 3B activated parameters.
The card emphasizes agentic coding, thinking preservation, long context, OpenAI-compatible serving, tool-call parser support, and benchmark rows for coding/general agent tasks.
The model supports vLLM, SGLang, KTransformers, Transformers serving, and Docker-style local app paths.
benchmark_implications: Track active parameters separately from total parameters.
Add thinking-preservation and tool-call-parser variables.
Measure serving framework compatibility as part of local model readiness.

sources.title: DeepSeek-V3.2: Efficient Reasoning & Agentic AI
observed_claims: DeepSeek-V3.2 presents sparse attention, scalable reinforcement learning, and large-scale agentic task synthesis as key methods.
The model card emphasizes efficient long-context reasoning and agent performance.
The card exposes local serving paths through vLLM, SGLang, Docker Model Runner, and quantization browsing.
benchmark_implications: Add long-context efficiency and agentic data-synthesis variables.
Measure whether claims are reproducible from public artifacts or remain vendor-reported.
Track sparse-attention/runtime assumptions separately from final benchmark scores.

sources.title: Kimi K2.6 quickstart
observed_claims: Kimi K2.6 is described as multimodal with stronger long-horizon coding, instruction compliance, self-correction, and agent execution.
The docs list text, image, and video input, thinking and non-thinking modes, dialogue and agent tasks, and multi-step tool invocation.
The docs include integration pages for Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, Cline, and RooCode.
benchmark_implications: Add harness-portability variables for Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode/OpenClaw, and Hermes-like agents.
Measure thinking/non-thinking mode selection and self-correction loops.
Track long-horizon coding stability and multimodal tool-use paths.

sources.title: GLM-5.2 model card
observed_claims: GLM-5.2 is positioned for long-horizon tasks with solid 1M-token context.
The card emphasizes flexible coding effort levels, IndexShare sparse-attention efficiency, MTP speculative decoding, and an MIT license.
The card provides vLLM, SGLang, Docker Model Runner, and quantization paths.
benchmark_implications: Add 1M-context stability and effort-level curves to model pressure tests.
Measure index/sparse-attention claims as runtime efficiency variables.
Track license openness and regional/deployment limitations explicitly.

sources.title: MiniMax-M2.5 model card
observed_claims: MiniMax describes M2.5 as trained with reinforcement learning in complex real-world environments.
The model card emphasizes coding, agentic tool use, search, office work, speed, and cost effectiveness.
The card includes sections for Forge agent-native RL, reward design, MiniMax Agent, local deployment, tool calling, and ModelScope.
benchmark_implications: Add cost-per-agent-hour and speed-under-agent-task variables.
Track RL environment diversity and reward-design claims as source-mined variables.
Add office-work and professional-employee task lanes.

sources.title: Hunyuan-A13B-Instruct model card
observed_claims: Hunyuan-A13B is an open-source fine-grained MoE model designed for efficient reasoning and general-purpose use.
The card reports competitive math, science, coding, instruction, and agent benchmark results.
The card documents thinking-mode controls through enable_thinking and /think or /no_think prompting.
benchmark_implications: Add small-active-parameter reasoning variables for local deployment.
Measure thinking-mode controls consistently across Chinese open-weight families.
Track agent benchmark coverage beyond SWE-style coding.

sources.title: VitaBench real-world interactive agent benchmark
observed_claims: VitaBench evaluates agents on real-world interactive tasks that require temporal/spatial reasoning, tool use, clarification, and shifting user-intent tracking.
The construction pipeline abstracts real-life scenarios into API tool graphs with preconditions, postconditions, and dependencies.
The benchmark reports a large gap between single-scenario and cross-scenario success rates.
benchmark_implications: Add dynamic user-intent and cross-domain composition variables.
Represent tools as typed graphs with preconditions and postconditions.
Use sliding-window rubrics for multi-path stochastic agent evaluation.

sources.title: WBench multi-turn interactive video world model benchmark
observed_claims: WBench is a multi-turn benchmark for interactive video world model evaluation.
The paper page says evaluation uses 22 automatic sub-metrics combining specialist vision models and large multimodal models, validated against human judgments.
The reported conclusion is that no single model is strong across all evaluated dimensions.
benchmark_implications: Use multiple diagnostic sub-metrics instead of one multimodal score.
Validate automatic metrics against human judgments where practical.
Expose model weakness profiles rather than only ranking.

sources.title: Personal AI Agents vs. Business Automation Agents: Hermes vs. Atom architecture feedback
observed_claims: Personal agents optimize for experiential memory, local freedom, procedural skill authoring, and developer-oriented terminal workflows.

Business automation agents require SQL or event state as source of truth, vector stores as acceleration layers, maturity gates, HITL, deterministic state machines, audit trails, and sandboxed skill evolution.
Skill evolution should be gated by maturity, unit tests, isolated sandboxes, and fitness scores across latency, token usage, and accuracy.
Canvas or UI state should be visible to the agent so human and agent can resolve errors from the same state.
benchmark_implications: Add source-of-truth versus retrieval-acceleration separation to memory benchmarks.
Add maturity-gated autonomy and HITL promotion variables.
Add sandboxed skill mutation and AlphaEvolver-style fitness scoring.
Add UI-state grounding and shared human-agent observability variables.

benchmark_lanes.description: Evaluate whether the engine can reason about production multimodal workflows where text, image, audio, video, local deployment, adapter training, workflow integration, and deployment boundaries matter more than demo output alone.
scenario_families: local desktop versus API versus on-prem deployment

benchmark_lanes.description: Evaluate whether local-model routing and benchmarks account for the current Chinese/open-weight model ecosystem, including agentic coding, long-context, thinking-mode, serving-framework, cost, and reproducibility variables.

Evaluate whether a self-improving agent can combine personal-agent freedom with business-grade determinism: SQL/event source-of-truth state, vector acceleration, maturity gates, HITL, sandboxed skill evolution, fitness scoring, UI-state grounding, and auditable receipts.
scenario_families: maturity promotion from read-only to autonomous
memento skill creation after repeated failure
